template:
Tu audites l application des décisions dans ce rendu IA.
Image 1 = photo source. Image 2 = rendu.

DÉCISIONS À VÉRIFIER :
{{decisionsJson}}

Pour chaque élément (mismatch_type "none" = conserver, "surface" = personnaliser) :
- "none" : l élément est-il visuellement présent et non altéré dans sa forme et matière ?
- "surface" : la modification demandée (action_slug) est-elle visuellement visible ?

Retourne UNIQUEMENT ce JSON (sans markdown) :
{"elements":[{"element_id":"e1","expected":"état cible attendu en une phrase","checks":[{"action_slug":"repaint","applied":true,"confidence":0.9}],"element_preserved":true,"notes":""}]}

Règles :
- Inclure UNIQUEMENT les element_id présents dans les décisions fournies
- element_preserved : true si l élément est reconnaissable et non remplacé dans le rendu
- checks : vide pour mismatch_type "none", une entrée par action_slug pour "surface"
- applied : true uniquement si la modification est CLAIREMENT visible
- confidence : 0 à 1 — certitude de ton évaluation
- Ne juge pas la qualité esthétique, uniquement la présence ou l absence des modifications

You are auditing an AI interior design render. Image 1 = source photo (original room). Image 2 = AI-generated styled render of the SAME room.

IMPORTANT: This is a style transformation. Walls, floors, furniture appearance, colors, and materials WILL intentionally change. Do NOT penalize for stylistic changes — they are expected and correct.

Evaluate only what should NOT change:
1. perspectiveMatch (0-10): camera angle and framing identical — same viewpoint, same lens
2. roomLayout (0-10): structural elements preserved — windows/doors positions, ceiling height, room proportions (NOT their finish or style)
3. photoRealism (0-10): does Image 2 look like a real interior photograph?
4. sameRoom: is Image 2 clearly the same physical space (not a completely different room)?

Return ONLY valid JSON:
{
  "perspectiveMatch": 0-10,
  "roomLayout": 0-10,
  "photoRealism": 0-10,
  "sameRoom": true/false,
  "issues": ["specific issues only, empty if none"],
  "overallPass": true/false
}

overallPass = true if: perspectiveMatch>=7 AND roomLayout>=7 AND photoRealism>=6 AND sameRoom=true.
notes: Audit qualité post-génération. Compare source et rendu. Score < 7 = rejet.

template:
This SINGLE image contains TWO photos placed SIDE BY SIDE: the LEFT half = AVANT (original photo), the RIGHT half = APRÈS (final render). Compare the LEFT half against the RIGHT half.

Focus ONLY on the main room in the foreground. IGNORE anything in an adjacent or background room, or seen through a door/window.

DESIGN INTENT (what the user actually decided — use it to avoid false positives):
{{intent}}
An element listed as KEPT, and not mentioned in the user live-edit requests, must NOT be reported as changed — the render redraws it with minor lighting/texture variation, but it is the SAME piece (not a purchase). Only override this if it is OBVIOUSLY a different object.

Inspect EACH zone below and COMPARE its appearance on the LEFT vs the RIGHT:
- Walls & ceiling: paint/color (incl. subtle shifts like white->beige), mouldings, panelling
- Floor: material or covering
- CEILING & LIGHTING (look up): ceiling/pendant light, chandelier, spots, wall sconce, floor lamp, table lamp
- Seating: sofa, armchair, chairs. Tables: coffee, side, dining. Storage: tv stand, dresser, sideboard, bookshelf
- WALL DECOR: frames, art, posters, mirrors
- TEXTILES: rug, curtains, cushions
- Plants & objects

Return ONLY a valid JSON object:
{
  "alterations": [
    {
      "element": "short label",
      "action": "added | replaced | restyled | painted | floor_changed | removed",
      "category": "sofa | armchair | chair | coffee_table | side_table | rug | floor | paint | mouldings | lamp | floor_lamp | tv_stand | bookshelf | dresser | nightstand | curtains | plant | other",
      "detail": "what specifically changed (before -> after)",
      "shoppingImpact": "none | to_buy | to_buy_secondhand | diy_material"
    }
  ]
}

DECISION RULE — be accurate, not exhaustive. Judge EACH item INDEPENDENTLY: a change to one item (e.g. the sofa turned yellow) does NOT mean a neighbouring item (e.g. an armchair) also changed — verify each one on its own. Differences of lighting, warmth, shadow or rendering sharpness are NOT changes; only a clearly different COLOR (e.g. grey -> green), material, shape or object counts.
- List an element ONLY if it is CLEARLY DIFFERENT between LEFT and RIGHT: replaced by another object, added, removed, repainted, re-covered, or restyled.
- If an element looks the SAME in both halves (e.g. the SAME rug, same sofa, same lamp, same wall color), DO NOT list it — even after checking it. Identical = not listed.
- When unsure whether it truly changed, leave it out.

shoppingImpact:
- new / replaced furniture, light fixture, rug, curtain, frame, decor -> "to_buy_secondhand" (or "to_buy").
- painted walls/ceiling, new flooring, reupholstered, stained wood -> "diy_material".
- removed or only moved -> "none".
One entry per real change, no duplicates.
notes: Compare source et rendu final, extrait la liste des changements pour alimenter la liste de courses. [backup extract 2026-06-16] [backup extract-v2 2026-06-16] [rewrite compare-strict 2026-06-17]

template:
You are a French interior design advisor. Look at the rendered image (attached) and generate a shopping list for the user.

STYLE: {{styleName}} — {{styleMood}}

DETECTED CHANGES (what was added/modified vs the original room):
{{alterationsJson}}

For each item with shoppingImpact "to_buy", "to_buy_secondhand", or "diy_material", generate one shopping entry. Look at the image to name the item precisely.

Return ONLY a valid JSON object:
{
  "shoppingList": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "name": "French product name, specific (e.g. 'Canapé 3 places en tissu naturel écru')",
      "category": "sofa|armchair|table|coffee_table|rug|lamp|tv_stand|paint|floor|molding|shelf|mirror|cushion|curtain|plant|other",
      "detail": "one line: material, color, style reference visible in the image",
      "priceMin": 80,
      "priceMax": 350,
      "source": "secondhand|new|diy|reuse",
      "merchants": [
        { "name": "MerchantName", "source": "secondhand|new|diy|reuse" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Source values:
- "secondhand": buying used (Selency, Leboncoin, Vinted, Troc de l'Île)
- "new": buying new (IKEA, La Redoute, Maisons du Monde, Alinéa, BUT)
- "diy": materials for DIY work (Leroy Merlin, Castorama, ManoMano)
- "reuse": keeping or repurposing an existing item — name it "Votre [meuble] actuel" or "Peindre l'existant"

Merchant rules (use ONLY these per source):
- "secondhand": [Selency, Leboncoin, Vinted, Troc de l'Île]
- "new": [IKEA, La Redoute, Maisons du Monde, Alinéa, BUT]
- "diy": [Leroy Merlin, Castorama, ManoMano]
- "reuse": describe the action as the name (e.g. "Votre canapé actuel refait", "Repeindre l'existant")

Per item: provide 2-3 merchants. For furniture REPLACED (action="replaced" in the alteration), add one "reuse" option last so the user can choose to keep and restyle their existing piece.

The item's top-level "source" = the source of the FIRST/recommended merchant.
Priority: secondhand > new > diy. Never set furniture primary source to "new" if secondhand makes sense.
priceMin/priceMax in euros, realistic for France (for the recommended option).
List only items where shoppingImpact is not "none".
notes: Shopping list from final render + alterations JSON. Returns shoppingList[] with name/category/detail/priceMin/priceMax/source/merchants.

template:
Analyse cette photo de pièce. Style cible : {{styleName}} — {{styleMood}}.

Actions DIY disponibles :
{{allActionsJson}}

Identifie les éléments significatifs et retourne UNIQUEMENT ce JSON (sans markdown) :
{"elements":[{"element_id":"e1","element":"nom","category":"sofa|armchair|chair|coffee_table|side_table|nightstand|dresser|bed|lamp|rug|plant|wall|floor|ceiling|window|door|other","description":"description visuelle courte","material_family":"fabric|wood|metal|stone|glass|plastic|leather|textile|ceramic|other|unknown","surface_features":[],"condition":"new|good|worn|damaged","movable":true,"dims":{},"mismatch_type":"none|surface|structural","action_slug":null,"action_label":null}]}

Règles :
- mismatch_type "none" : s intègre au style cible → conserver
- mismatch_type "surface" : forme OK mais surface à transformer → choisir l action la plus pertinente dont applies_to_categories contient la catégorie de l élément
- mismatch_type "structural" : incompatible avec le style → remplacer
- action_slug et action_label : renseigner uniquement si mismatch_type="surface" et qu une action correspondante existe
- dims : inclure uniquement les dimensions estimables depuis la photo
- element_id : séquence e1, e2, e3…
- Ignorer les petits accessoires (coussins isolés, bibelots, cadres)

Analyze this interior room photo. Return ONLY a JSON object listing what you observe. Do not give any instruction or recommendation — only describe what is present.

{
  "roomType": "salon | chambre | autre",
  "architecture": {
    "floor": "material and condition (e.g. 'worn light wood parquet')",
    "walls": "color, material, condition",
    "ceiling": "description",
    "windows": "count, size, position, light direction",
    "doors": "count, position"
  },
  "detectedElements": [
    {
      "element": "short label (e.g. 'staircase', 'wall mirror', 'fireplace', 'radiator', 'sofa')",
      "type": "furniture | architectural | fixture | decor",
      "location": "where in the room",
      "description": "color, material, style, condition",
      "movable": true
    }
  ],
  "qualityWarnings": ["e.g. 'too dark', 'blurry', 'partially cropped'"]
}

Rules:
- List EVERY visible element: furniture AND architectural features AND fixtures.
- "movable": true for furniture, false for built-in/structural (staircase, fireplace, radiator).
- Describe only. Do NOT decide what to keep or change.
- Return ONLY the JSON.
notes: Détection pure : Vision ne décide jamais, il observe.
